objective: Build a tiny content-addressed greeting library. ARTIFACT: a zero-dependency Node.js >= 18 ESM library (no CLI, no services, no network, no database, no ledger, no persistence) in out/greet.mjs (implementation) and out/index.mjs (public re-exports). API CONTRACT: createGreeting(name) derives text = `Hello, ${name}!`, computes id, stores the greeting in a module-local singleton Map keyed by id, and returns { id, text }; name MUST be a non-empty string of at most 200 characters, otherwise createGreeting throws TypeError deterministically (no coercion); duplicate content is idempotent - the same name yields the same id and a single stored entry, never an error. contentId(greeting) returns the id for greeting.text and throws TypeError if greeting is not an object with a string text property. getGreeting(id) returns the stored greeting object or undefined for any unknown, non-string, or malformed id (never throws). listGreetings() returns a new array in insertion order of shallow copies, so callers cannot mutate the store. CONTENT ADDRESSING (exact): id = lowercase hex sha256 (node:crypto) of the UTF-8 bytes of JSON.stringify({ text }) - a single-key object, so key ordering is trivially canonical; no trailing newline or whitespace. PINNED CONFORMANCE FIXTURE: createGreeting('world') MUST produce text 'Hello, world!' with canonical form {"text":"Hello, world!"} and id 1f91dd9b455c3dd93303d1fce05833f8b953d64323ecc86ecb612e4861f8dfcc; the acceptance test MUST assert this exact hex constant, plus retrieval by id, idempotent duplicates, insertion-order listing, and the TypeError cases; every file passes node --check. The test is re-run independently by the verifying gate (not only by its author). PROCESS NOTE (enclosing pipeline, NOT part of the artifact): the build runs sandboxed - teams write only their declared files, with no credentials, network, or publishing; verification, audit, and rollback are responsibilities of the surrounding TELOS pipeline, and the artifact itself carries none of that governance surface.
